references.title: Modeling Knowledge Acquisition from Multiple Learning Resource Types

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Score on Next Graded Learning Material
extracted.Courses: Each dataset refers to one course
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models:
Current knowledge models tend to avoid relationships between different types of learning materials. 

Current measures (such as time spent on specific resources) may indicate contradictory realities.
extracted.Novelty of Model:
The proposed model identifies relationships between different types of learning resources while, simultaneously, building a domain knowledge model that allows a better mapping of materials to their respective knowledge components. 

This model follows an essential assumption: the knowledge provided by different types of learning materials can be described using the same latent space → meaning transferrability between types of learning materials.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Each learning material as pre-assigned to a specific knowledge components.

Learners are represented by a tensor of 3-lower dimensional representations:

1. Latent Features

2. A temporal dynamic knowledge tensor

3. A mapping between materials and different knowledge concepts.

The preoduct between Knowledge Tensor T and Latent Feature _Matrix S represents the student’s knowledge in each concept before an attempt.
extracted.Models:
Individual BKT,
DKT
Deedback-Driven Tensor Factorization, 
Tensor Factorization Without Learning,
Bayesian Probabilistic Tensor Factorization, 
Average of All Predictions, 
Multi-View Knowledge Model
extracted.Comments:
For each model, the probability is assumed to be the predicted score on each exercise.

To do that, the authors benchmark their models in 6 distinct datasets: 3 synthetic datasets and 3 real datasets.

Tests include some form of hyper-parameter tuning via Grid Search. Model includes constraint for learning and forgetting materials. Results show that these constraints, while not universal, are important constructs to consider in knowledge modelling,
extracted.Limitations: All datasets are done on a MOOC/course specific analysis.

annotations.text: Students learn from multiple resources and an effective learner representation should probably account for them as it would allow for an effective detection of knowledge gaps and recommendation of learning materials. KT traditionally only uses problems for this modelling, ignoring other forms of materials.

The paper proposes a KT model that incorporates data from multiple resource types - Multiview Knowledge Model (MVKM) using tensor factorization - which is meant to be able to reduce dimensionality. The authors perform multiple tests for predictive accuracy and curiously the authors found multiple similarities across different types of learning materials.
